column1: Form EIA-923, Power Plant Operations Report and instructions

column2: EIA Plant Identification number. One to five digit numeric.
Whether or not the plant is a combined heat & power facility (cogenerator).One character alphanumeric, “Y” or “N”
For nuclear plants only, the unit number .One digit numeric. Nuclear plants are the only type of plants for which data are shown explicitly at the generating unit level.
The name of the entity which operates the plant. Alphanumeric.
The EIA operator identification number. Five digit numeric, padded with leading zeros.
State the facility is located in. Two character alphanumeric (standard state postal codes).
The census region the state is located in.Three to five character alphanumeric:
The North American Electric Reliability Council (NERC) region where the plant is located.Alphanumeric:
The facility’s North American Industrial Classification System (NAICS) code.Numeric. For information on the NAICS system, see:http://www.census.gov/epcd/www/naics.html

column1: EIA Sector Number and Sector Name
column2: EIA’s internal consolidated NAICS sectors.For internal purposes, EIA consolidates the NAICS categories into seven groups.  These are shown below in the Sector Codes and Names table:
Electric Utility: Traditional regulated electric utilities
NAICS-22 Non-Cogen: Independent power producers which are not cogenerators
NAICS-22 Cogen: Independent power producers which are cogenerators, but whose primary business purpose is the sale of electricity to the public
Commercial NAICS Non-Cogen: Commercial non-cogeneration facilities that produce electric power, are connected to the gird, and can sell power to the public
Commercial NAICS Cogen: Commercial cogeneration facilities that produce electric power, are connected to the grid, and can sell power to the public
Industrial NAICS Non-Cogen: Industrial non-cogeneration facilities that produce electric power, are connected to the gird, and can sell power to the public
Industrial NAICS Cogen: Industrial cogeneration facilities that produce electric power, are connected to the gird, and can sell power to the public
Turbines Used in a Binary Cycle. Including those used for geothermal applications
Combined-Cycle Single-Shaft Combustion Turbine and Steam Turbine share of single generator
Energy Storage, Other (Specify on Schedule 9, Comments)
Combustion (Gas) Turbine. Including Jet Engine design
Hydraulic Turbine. Including turbines associated with delivery of water by pipeline.
Energy Storage, Reversible Hydraulic Turbine (Pumped Storage)
Steam Turbine. Including Nuclear, Geothermal, and Solar Steam (does not include Combined Cycle).
The fuel code reported to EIA.Two or three letter alphanumeric:
Distillate Fuel Oil. Including diesel, No. 1, No. 2, and No. 4 fuel oils.
Nuclear. Including Uranium, Plutonium, and Thorium.
Other Biomass Gas. Including digester gas, methane, and other biomass gases.
Residual Fuel Oil. Including No. 5 & 6 fuel oils and bunker C fuel oil.
Coal-based Synfuel. Including briquettes, pellets, or extrusions, which are formed by binding materials or processes that recycle materials.

Water at a Conventional Hydroelectric Turbine and water used in Wave Buoy Hydrokinetic Technology, current Hydrokinetic Technology, Tidal Hydrokinetic Technology, and Pumping Energy for Reversible (Pumped Storage) Hydroelectric Turbines.
Waste/Other Coal. Including anthracite culm, bituminous gob, fine coal, lignite waste, waste coal.
Wood Waste Liquids, excluding Black Liquor. Including red liquor, sludge wood, spent sulfite liquor, and other wood-based liquids.
Wood/Wood Waste Solids. Including paper pellets, railroad ties, utility polies, wood chips, bark, and other wood waste solids.
Waste Heat not directly attributed to a fuel source
Waste/Other Oil. Including crude oil, liquid butane, liquid propane, naphtha, oil waste, re-refined moto oil, sludge oil, tar oil, or other petroleum-based liquid wastes.

A partial aggregation of the reported fuel type codes into larger categories used by EIA in, for example, the Monthly Energy Review (MER).Two or three letter alphanumeric.  See the Fuel Code table (Table 5), below:
Biogenic Municipal Solid Waste and Landfill Gas
The type of physical units fuel consumption is reported in:
Consumption of the fuel type in physical units.Numeric.  Note: this is the total quantity consumed for both electricity and, in the case of combined heat and power plants, process steam production.
Consumption for electric generation of the fuel type in physical units.Numeric.
Heat content of the fuel in millions of Btus per physical unit.Numeric.

Total consumption of the fuel in millions of Btus.Numeric   Note: this is the total quantity consumed for both electricity and, in the case of combined heat and power plants, process steam production.

Consumption of fuel in millions of Btus for the purpose of generating electricity.These fields are most relevant to combined heat and power plants.  For non-combined heat and power plants, the data in these fields and in columns AK through AV (total fuel consumed) will be identical.

Net generation of electricity in megawatthours (MWh).Numeric.  This is total electrical output net of station service.  In the case of combined heat and power plants, this value is intended to include internal consumption of electricity for the purposes of a production process, as well as power put on the grid.

Total consumption of fuel in physical units, year to date.Numeric   Note: this is the total quantity consumed for both electricity and, in the case of combined heat and power plants, process steam production.
Total consumption of fuel to produce electricity, in physical units, year to date. Numeric

Total consumption of fuel in MMBtus, year to date. Numeric   Note: this is the total quantity consumed for both electricity and, in the case of combined heat and power plants, process steam production.

Consumption of fuel in millions of Btus for the purpose of generating electricity, year to date. This field is most relevant to combined heat and power plants.  For non-combined heat and power plants, the data in this field and in column BV (total fuel consumed) will be identical.

Net generation, year to date in megawatthours (MWh).Numeric. This is total electrical output net of station service.  In the case of combined heat and power plants, this value is intended to include internal consumption of electricity for the purposes of a production process, as well as power put on the grid.
The census region and state the facility is located in
Petroleum Liquids stock in Thousand Barrels
Petroleum Coke stock in Thousand Barrels
EIA Plant Identification number. One to five digit numeric.
Whether or not the plant is a combined heat & power facility (cogenerator). One character alphanumeric, “Y” or “N”
The name of the entity which operates the plant. Alphanumeric.
The EIA operator identification number. Five digit numeric, padded with leading zeros.
State the facility is located in.Two character alphanumeric (standard state postal codes).
The census region the state is located in.Three to five character alphanumeric:
The North American Electric Reliability Council (NERC) region where the plant is located.Alphanumeric:
The facility’s North American Industrial Classification System (NAICS) code.Numeric.  For information on the NAICS system, see:http://www.census.gov/epcd/www/naics.html

The type of physical units fuel consumption is reported in. All consumption is reported in either short tons for solids, thousands of cubic feet for gases, and barrels for liquids:

Consumption of the fuel type specified in Column N, in physical units.Numeric.  Note: this is the total quantity consumed for both electricity and, in the case of combined heat and power plants, process steam production
Heat content of the fuel in millions of Btus per physical unit to the nearest 0.001. Numeric
Sulfur content percentage by weight to the nearest 0.01 percent
Ash content percentage by weight to the nearest 0.1 percent

Total consumption of fuel in physical units, year to date.Numeric   Note: this is the total quantity consumed for both electricity and, in the case of combined heat and power plants, process steam production.

Three character code designating the regulatory
Non-Regulated Independent Power Plant or Nonutility Plant.
The name of the utility or company that operates the plant. Alphanumeric.
One character designates the reporting frequency for the plant. Alphanumeric:
Mode for the longest / second longest distance.
Rail: Shipments of fuel moved to consumers by rail (private or public/commercial). Included is coal hauled to or away from a railroad siding by truck if the truck did not use public roads.
River:  Shipments of fuel moved to consumers via river by barge.  Not included are shipments to Great Lakes coal loading docks, tidewater piers, or coastal ports.

Great Lakes:  Shipments of coal moved to consumers via the Great Lakes. These shipments are moved via the Great Lakes coal loading docks, which are identified by name and location as follows:  Conneaut Coal Storage & Transfer, Conneaut, Ohio;  NS Coal Dock (Ashtabula Coal Dock), Ashtabula, Ohio;  Sandusky Coal Pier, Sandusky, Ohio;  Toledo Docks, Toledo, Ohio; KCBX Terminals Inc., Chicago, Illinois;  Superior Midwest Energy Terminal, Superior, Wisconsin

Tidewater Piers and Coastal Ports:  Shipments of coal moved to Tidewater Piers and Coastal Ports for further shipments to consumers via coastal water or ocean.  The Tidewater Piers and Coastal Ports are identified by name and location as follows:  Dominion Terminal Associates, Newport News, Virginia; McDuffie Coal Terminal, Mobile, Alabama;  IC Railmarine Terminal, Convent, Louisiana;  International Marine Terminals, Myrtle Grove, Louisiana;  Cooper/T. Smith Stevedoring Co. Inc., Darrow, Louisiana;  Seward Terminal Inc., Seward, Alaska;  Los Angeles Export Terminal, Inc., Los Angeles, California;  Levin-Richmond Terminal Corp., Richmond, California; Baltimore Terminal, Baltimore, Maryland;  Norfolk Southern Lamberts Point P-6, Norfolk, Virginia;  Chesapeake Bay Piers, Baltimore, Maryland;  Pier IX Terminal Company, Newport News, Virginia;  Electro-Coal Transport Corp., Davant, Louisiana
Water: Shipments of fuel moved to consumers by other waterways.
Truck: Shipments of fuel moved to consumers by truck.  Not included is fuel hauled to or away from a railroad siding by truck on non-public roads.
Tramway/Conveyor: Shipments of fuel moved to consumers by tramway or conveyor.
Slurry Pipeline: Shipments of coal moved to consumers by slurry pipeline.
Pipeline: Shipments of fuel moved to consumers by pipeline

Chlorine content in parts per million (ppm) of the coal quantity. Numeric.

State the facility is located in. Two character alphanumeric (standard state postal codes).
